articles.title: 'Garmi ka ilaaj, jeb mein rakho pyaaz': Congress's jibe at Delhi govt's heatwave campaign
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Delhi
articles.full_text:
'Garmi ka ilaaj, jeb mein rakho pyaaz': Congress's jibe at Delhi govt's heatwave campaign
The Congress on Thursday took a swipe at Delhi chief minister over her heatwave campaign, saying a minister in the Modi government has already announced a heat action plan of Garmi ka ilaaj, jeb mein rakho pyaaz. The dig by Congress leader and former environment minister Jairam Ramesh was at Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindias recently shared age-old remedy of keeping an onion in his pocket, which he claimed he uses to beat the heat during peak summer.
The Congress on Thursday took a swipe at Delhi chief minister over her heatwave campaign, saying a minister in the Modi government has already announced a heat action plan of ''Garmi ka ilaaj, jeb mein rakho pyaaz.'' The dig by Congress leader and former environment minister Jairam Ramesh was at Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia's recently shared ''age-old remedy'' of keeping an onion in his pocket, which he claimed he uses to beat the heat during peak summer. ''The Delhi CM has just announced a heat action plan for the nation's capital with a campaign theme of 'Garmi se jung, Delhi Sarkar ke sang'. ''Actually, her BJP colleague and a Minister of the Modi Government had already launched a heat action plan with the theme 'Garmi ka ilaaj, jeb mein rakho pyaaz','' Ramesh said in a post on X. Addressing a gathering in his home constituency, Guna in Madhya Pradesh, a few days ago, Scindia said that in today's age, everyone carries phones, but he carries an onion, as it helps regulate body temperature in scorching heat. He then pulled out an onion from his pocket to prove his point, much to the amusement of the gathering. ''I don't use an AC in my car, I just carry an onion in my pocket,'' Scindia said. ''Keep an onion in your pocket; nothing will happen even in 51-degree heat,'' the Union communications minister added.
(This story has not been edited by Devdiscourse staff and is auto-generated from a syndicated feed.)

articles.title: Delhi Govt Orders 1-4 PM Break For Outdoor Workers Amid Heatwave - BW Businessworld
articles.full_text:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has announced a series of coordinated measures to protect residents from the ongoing heatwave, with a specific focus on labourers and outdoor workers who face the highest exposure to extreme temperatures.
Highlighting the serious health risks posed by prolonged exposure to heat, the Chief Minister said that all workers in both the private and public sectors must be given a mandatory break between 1 PM and 4 PM, the peak heat hours. The directive aims to reduce direct sun exposure and prevent heat-related illnesses, such as heatstroke and dehydration, among those working outdoors for extended periods.
As part of efforts to ensure adequate hydration for the public, the government has arranged drinking water facilities across key public spaces. Water provisions have been introduced on buses operated by the Delhi Transport Corporation, while volunteers are being deployed at bus shelters to distribute water and Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) to passengers. These steps are designed to provide immediate relief to daily commuters affected by the extreme weather.
The healthcare system in the city has also been placed on alert. Hospitals have been stocked with essential medicines and are prepared to handle cases related to heat stress. Cooling areas are being created within hospital premises to offer quick respite to patients suffering from heat-related conditions.
In schools, authorities have been directed to ensure the availability of drinking water and ORS for students to safeguard children during school hours. Additionally, volunteers are distributing caps and cotton mufflers at multiple locations to help residents shield themselves from the harsh sun.
The Chief Minister stressed that public awareness and timely precautions remain critical in reducing the impact of the heatwave and urged residents to remain cautious during peak daytime hours.

articles.title: Delhi CM reviews heatwave measures and plans citywide awareness campaign - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta chaired a high-level meeting on Friday to evaluate preparedness under the Heat Wave Action Plan 2026 and issued specific, time-bound orders to all departments to enhance measures at every level.
With temperatures rising and heatwave conditions worsening, Gupta highlighted the government’s target to eliminate heatwave-related deaths by 2030.
The meeting involved Chief Secretary Rajiv Verma and senior officials from relevant departments. Gupta instructed hospitals to ensure sufficient bed capacity for heat-related cases and ambulances to be fully equipped for heat emergencies.
She also called for a comprehensive citywide awareness campaign to educate residents on preventive measures. Schools have been directed to implement a ‘water bell’ system reminding students to drink water regularly to prevent dehydration.
Gupta further mandated the provision of clean drinking water at hospitals, schools, construction sites, Atal canteens, police stations, post offices, and Delhi Transport Corporation bus stands.
She stated, “ORS must be accessible at all major public locations. Water tankers, water coolers and water ATMs should function seamlessly across the city. In addition, adequate arrangements for shade and drinking water for animals and birds must be ensured.”
The Heat Action Plan 2026 is executed by the Delhi Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), coordinating multiple departments and collaborating with over 17 knowledge partners, including NGOs, to ensure a scientifically robust and synchronised response.
Gupta noted that Delhi has encountered record temperatures recently, reaching 43.7°C in 2025 and surpassing 40°C for nearly 40 days during the last two to three years. A comprehensive science-based framework has been established in response.
The India Meteorological Department’s colour-coded alert system—green, yellow, orange, and red—is utilised for continuous monitoring, with real-time alerts issued to departments and the public.
Regarding health measures, more than 339 health centres across Delhi are stocked with ORS, ice packs, and supplies. Over 30 hospitals have dedicated ‘cool rooms’ for heatstroke management, and 174 medical officers and ASHA workers have been trained to manage heat-related illnesses. Additionally, 330 ambulances are kept on standby.
In terms of public infrastructure, drinking water points, water coolers, shaded shelters, and cooling points are being installed citywide. Delhi Police have deployed over 11,000 air coolers and more than 1,900 water coolers, while the traffic police distribute ORS packets extensively, with plans to expand the campaign.
Gupta emphasised protecting construction workers and other vulnerable groups by adjusting working hours to avoid peak afternoon heat. Employers must provide shaded areas, rest breaks, drinking water, and ORS at worksites to prevent heat-related health risks.
She directed all departments to cooperate closely and ensure effective implementation of the plan to shield Delhi residents from the severe effects of the continuing heatwave.
